channel.title: Jeremydmiller.com on .NET Ramblings
channel.description: Recent content in Jeremydmiller.com on .NET Ramblings

item.title: Result pattern or throwing exceptions? Wolverine says “neither”
item.description: Just a warning, unless a topic is personal, I’m going to start mostly writing on the JasperFx Software website to try to drive more traffic there. Don’t throw exceptions for an entity that isn’t found or a value out of range — fair. But swapping that for Result everywhere just trades one kind of noise … Continue reading Result pattern or throwing exceptions? Wolverine says “neither” → Link to article: https://jeremydmiller.

item.title: The Codebase Is the Prompt: Wolverine, Vertical Slices, and AI-Assisted Development
item.description: I’m taking a little bit of time today to start writing up a “strategery” document on the JasperFx / Critter Stack approach to AI in the near future. One of the arguments I’m wanting to lean into quite heavily to call Wolverine AI-friendly is how it compresses code in its approach to “Vertical Slice Architecture” … Continue reading The Codebase Is the Prompt: Wolverine, Vertical Slices, and AI-Assisted Development →

item.title: Wolverine Hit Five Million Downloads
item.description: I’m having an aggravating day at work today, so just indulge me in writing this up for fun whilst I’m waiting for some very slow CI builds to finish… If you’re not familiar with Wolverine, it’s a series of application frameworks for server side .NET development including asynchronous messaging, asynchronous processing via in process messaging, … Continue reading Wolverine Hit Five Million Downloads → Link to article: https://jeremydmiller.com/2026/06/03/wolverine-hit-five-million-downloads/

item.title: Wolverine and Marten Just Got Better for F# Folks
item.description: Polecat 4.0 shares many more internals with Marten and I’m hopeful that it’s also much better for F# developers as well. Wolverine and more so Marten do already have F# users, but we just made the deployment story a lot better in both tools for F# developers. One of the key components of Wolverine especially … Continue reading Wolverine and Marten Just Got Better for F# Folks → Link to article: https://jeremydmiller.
